perturbation.original_text:
5.1.2 Manufacturer will invoice EMV for Products net ten (10) days from when the parties sign the Quality Evaluation Form to confirm delivery of Products.

5.1.3 EMV shall pay 70% of total amount of a Purchase Order within ten (10) days of receipt of Manufacturer's invoice as provided in Section 5.1.2 above.
perturbation.changed_text:
5.1.2 Manufacturer will invoice EMV for Products net ten (10) days from when the parties sign the Quality Evaluation Form to confirm delivery of Products.

5.1.3 EMV shall pay 70% of total amount of a Purchase Order within forty-five (45) days of receipt of Manufacturer's invoice as provided in Section 5.1.2 above.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the payment deadline in Section 5.1.3 to 'forty-five (45) days' while Section 5.1.2 refers to payment within 'ten (10) days'. This creates confusion as to when the final payment is actually due, creating uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1.3 EMV shall pay 70% of total amount of a Purchase Order within forty-five (45) days of receipt of Manufacturer's invoice as provided in Section 5.1.2 above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 5.1.3 to state that EMV shall pay 70% of the total amount of a purchase order within 'forty-five (45) days' of receipt of the invoice. However, this directly contradicts the original intent and implicit meaning of Section 5.1.2, which establishes the baseline for payment based on the Quality Evaluation Form signing and implies a ten-day period. The contradiction lies in the incompatible payment deadlines between the two sections, creating uncertainty and potential disputes about when payment is actually due. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction,' where conflicting deadlines in different sections lead to in-text contradiction.
